changes: New platforms added to the site will now automatically appear in your feed turned on — even if you previously customized which sources to show.
Your saved platform preferences were reset this update due to an internal format change. Head to the Connections hub to set them again — sorry for the inconvenience.
Added a "What's new" button in the top-right corner so you can re-read past updates at any time.
Update notifications now appear in a cleaner, more structured format.
Telegram media now loads noticeably faster — responses are cached on the proxy server and served instantly on repeat visits.
Only one video or audio track can play at a time — starting a new one automatically pauses the previous.
Photo carousels in the feed now have previous/next navigation buttons and dot indicators — you can scroll through albums without leaving the page.
The Telegram media proxy now sends long-term cache headers, so your browser won't re-download media files it has already loaded.
Telegram videos can now be seeked to any point — the media proxy supports HTTP range requests and streams only the portion the player actually needs.
